acceptance.rule: has_axis {value_profile, value_profile_cmplog} >= lacks_axis {cmplog, naive} at every OPERAND_WIDTH; near-parity at OPERAND_WIDTH=1; at OPERAND_WIDTH=8 has_axis>0 while naive=0 and cmplog=0 (mask defeats I2S so cmplog cannot paste the goal)

design_basis: VP's CMP_MAP Hamming gradient on a single integer == is the only fitness signal for partial matches; its advantage scales with the number of bytes the candidate word must climb (1/256^W blind-search collapse for naive, ~constant climb for VP). The compared operands are XORed with a per-execution mask derived from a separate input region, so cmplog observes a moving CMP pair with no stable literal to substitute (I2S-unsubstitutable) while the candidate's Hamming distance to the goal is mask-invariant, preserving VP's gradient.
notes: OPERAND_WIDTH=1: 1-byte gradient; all four fuzzers expected near-parity (256-value space; naive havoc finds it quickly).
OPERAND_WIDTH=2: 16-bit gradient; VP {value_profile, value_profile_cmplog} pull ahead of {naive, cmplog}.
OPERAND_WIDTH=4: 32-bit gradient (matches member harfbuzz_9584/10156 codepoint width); naive ~0 by chance, cmplog blind because mask blocks I2S paste, VP climbs steadily.
OPERAND_WIDTH=8: 64-bit gradient; naive=0 and cmplog=0; only the has_axis (VP) fuzzers reach the trap.
cmplog parity with naive (not with value_profile) at high width is the load-bearing observation for the value_profile_cmplog>cmplog member: it isolates CMP_MAP gradient from I2S substitution.

screen._note: screen-tuning experiment (2026-05-24): override for the fast re-screen; honest grid stays in scan_values above
